serviceOverview.description: Orchestrates ephemeral Docker containers to execute LLM-driven code generation tasks based on design metadata.

dashboards.name: Agentic Harness - Primary Health
dashboards.description: Displays active container counts, Vault token lease errors, and LLM API latency.

dashboards.name: Agentic Harness - LLM Rate Limits
dashboards.description: Tracks consumption against our external vendor API limits.

alerts.triggerCondition: > 5% of new agent containers fail to retrieve a Vault token within 10 seconds.
alerts.immediateAction: Check the HashiCorp Vault cluster health dashboard. If Vault is healthy, proceed to Failure Mode FM-002.

alerts.triggerCondition: > 10 API requests per minute return a 429 status from the external LLM provider.
alerts.immediateAction: Verify if a massive batch ingestion job was triggered. Throttle the upstream ingestion webhook if necessary.

knownFailureModes.symptom: Agents are spinning up but timing out or receiving 429s from the LLM provider.
probableCauses: Spike in design publish events overloading our quota tier
investigationSteps: Run `kubectl logs -l app=agent-orchestrator -n code-gen-prod | grep '429'` to confirm rate limiting.
recoverySteps: If vendor is degraded: Enable the 'Fallback Model' feature flag in LaunchDarkly (flag key: `agent-llm-fallback-enabled`).
If quota exceeded: Access the AWS API Gateway console and temporarily reduce the global rate limit on the ingestion webhook to queue events rather than process them immediately.
verificationSteps: Observe the Datadog LLM Rate Limits dashboard; 429 errors should drop to 0 within 2 minutes.

knownFailureModes.symptom: Agent pods are crashing in a CrashLoopBackOff state due to missing secrets.
probableCauses: Vault proxy sidecar failing to authenticate with the Kubernetes service account.
investigationSteps: Describe a failing pod: `kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n code-gen-prod`.
Check Vault sidecar logs: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -c vault-agent -n code-gen-prod`.
recoverySteps: Restart the Vault injector deployment: `kubectl rollout restart deployment vault-agent-injector -n vault-system`.
verificationSteps: Watch the namespace: `kubectl get pods -n code-gen-prod -w`. Ensure new pods reach the 'Running' state.

procedure: A cronjob runs at 02:00 UTC to kill containers older than 4 hours.
If the cronjob fails (alert triggers), manually run the cleanup script located at `scripts/ops/prune_zombies.sh`.
